user_message: I need the team view on executive brief. I think there's an opportunity here but need to validate.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Eric Kim <eric.kim@cohowinery.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-22 15:58 EST
Subject: Follow-up: executive brief Review

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with executive brief. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Eric Kim <eric.kim@cohowinery.com>
Date: 2024-02-20 09:36 EST
Subject: Re: executive brief Discussion - Next Steps

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about executive brief. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Eric Kim <eric.kim@cohowinery.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-22 11:44 EST
Subject: URGENT: executive brief Issue at Coho Winery

There's been a change in our timeline for executive brief. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

Meeting: executive brief Discussion - Coho Winery
Date: 2024-02-23
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Eric Kim (Coho Winery), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on executive brief from our last session.

[00:07:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on resource allocation and found that there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope.

[00:12:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): Let me add some context -- we've been working on budget approval and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:16:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): On the executive brief front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around technical validation.

[00:18:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): On the executive brief front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around timeline constraints.

[00:23:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): The timeline for integration complexity is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:27:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on stakeholder alignment and found that there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us.

[00:32:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on cost optimization and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:37:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): On the executive brief front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around stakeholder alignment.

[00:39:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): The timeline for data residency is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:42:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is budget approval.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-K trap (One-page constraint hurts complex meetings); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-K failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-K failure mode (One-page constraint hurts complex meetings)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct executive brief decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-K -- One-page constraint hurts complex meetings
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
